Motion For Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-04-2022 Off Calendar

Motion For Judgment (Per Order Filed On January 10, 2022)
Off Calendar Apr-04-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. MICHAEL T. RISHER, Esq. of Risher Law (Tel: 510-589-1657) for plaintiff First Amendment Coalition;
Atty. THOMAS R. BURKE, Esq. of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP (Tel: 415-276-6552) for plaintiff KQED;
Atty. JOHN D. ECHEVERRIA, Esq. of California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 415-510-3479) for defendant California Department of Justice.

Hearing Re: Motion For Judgment (Per Order Filed On January 10, 2022).

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs filed this writ action in 2019 seeking records disclosure under California's then-new Senate Bill 1421, codified in Penal Code 832.7. The state's attorney general and department of justice have now produced more than 76,000 pages of documents and extensive privilege logs.

In a stipulation and order, the parties and this court agreed that the latter would address two remaining disclosure issues. Those two limited issues involved documents withheld by the department's civil rights enforcement section (CRES) regarding (1) Bakersfield police and (2) juveniles. (1/10/22 Stip. & Order 2:15-3:12; Pltf. Memo. 6:25-7:10.) As to the first issue, defendants say they are "in possession of records obtained in the course of certain pattern-or-practice investigations, which the Department received in response to investigative subpoenas issued under Government Code section 11181." (Dft. Memo. 13:24-26.) As to the second issue, defendants say they "located certain responsive records that were obtained by CRES in the course of certain pattern-or-practice investigations." (Id. at 17:16-17.) Absent further explanation of the records and the investigations (e.g., what "pattern-or-practice investigations" are and what they entailed in these specific instances), the court cannot rule.

The Court will refer to this case to the Presiding Judge to be reassigned as a singly-assigned case.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
